layer_1_flower.intent: Laser-focused creative energy with forest clarity

layer_2_essential_oil.blend_rationale: Tea Tree carries the dominant terpinolene. Sweet Basil matches the pine needles aroma note. Black Pepper provides β-Caryophyllene support. Lemongrass rounds the blend with a soft finish.

layer_3_music.spotify_prompt: A high-energy session. Laser-focused creative energy with forest clarity. Genres: funk, afrobeat, disco, nu-jazz. Tempo around 100-128 BPM.

layer_4_tea.name: XJ-13 Tea
layer_4_tea.description: Pine Needles and citrus infused green tea blend
method: Heat 8 oz filtered water to 175°F.
Add green tea tea bag and steep for 3-4 minutes.
Add Douglas fir needle tips and let infuse for 1 minute.
Add yuzu zest and let infuse for 1 minute.
Strain into a warm teacup.
Stir in honey and serve.

mocktail.name: XJ-13 Fizz
method: Fill a highball glass with large ice cubes.
Combine 2 oz pine needle syrup and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker with ice.
Add 0.5 oz fresh citrus juice blend.
Shake for 8 seconds.
Strain into glass over fresh ice.
Top with 2 oz sparkling water and stir gently.
Garnish with pine needles.

cocktail.name: XJ-13 Collins
cocktail.description: Blanco Tequila-based pine needles and citrus cocktail
method: Combine 2 oz blanco tequila, 1.5 oz pine needle syrup, and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker.
Add 0.5 oz fresh citrus juice blend.
Add ice.
Add ice and shake for 10 seconds.
Strain into a chilled highball.
Garnish with pine needles.

juice.name: XJ-13 Juice
method: Press 2 cups fresh apple through a cold press.
Press 1 cup orange through the press.
Add 0.5 inch fresh ginger root to the press.
Combine all juices in a glass.
Add 0.5 oz raw honey and stir until dissolved.
Pour over ice and serve immediately.

wellness_shot.name: XJ-13 Shot
method: Chill a shot glass in the freezer for 2 minutes.
In a mixing glass, combine 1 oz tequila with 0.75 oz fresh apple juice.
Add 0.25 oz fresh lemon juice.
Stir with ice for 5 seconds.
Strain into the chilled shot glass and serve immediately.

snack.name: Edamame with Chili Flakes & Sea Salt
snack.desc: Steamed edamame tossed with chili oil, flaky salt, and toasted sesame.

appetizer.name: Spring Rolls with Peanut Sauce
appetizer.desc: Fresh rice paper rolls with herbs, vermicelli, and spicy peanut dipping sauce.

main.name: Thai Green Curry with Fresh Vegetables
main.desc: Coconut-based green curry with seasonal vegetables, Thai basil, and jasmine rice.

dessert.name: Key Lime Pie Bars
dessert.desc: Tangy key lime curd on graham cracker crust with whipped cream.

cheese.name: Queso Fresco & Grilled Fruit
cheese.desc: Crumbled queso fresco with grilled peaches, honey, and pepitas.

bath_bomb.base: baking soda, citric acid, cornstarch, Epsom salt
bath_bomb.extras: dried flower petals, oat milk powder

shower_steamer.activation: Place on shower floor away from direct stream. Steam activates essential oils for 5-10 minutes of aromatherapy.

layer_10_incense.burn_method: Place on heat-resistant surface in a ventilated room. Allow 30 seconds for the flame to establish before extinguishing to a smolder.
layer_10_incense.ritual_suggestion: Light before the session begins. Let the smoke settle the room while you prepare your laser-focused space.

layer_11_environment.spatial_setup: Open space, natural light when possible. Standing or high-top surfaces. Plants visible.
layer_11_environment.scent_anchor: Essential oil blend via diffuser, started 20 minutes before session

layer_12_breathwork.timing: Begin 5 minutes before the session. Maintain rhythm throughout opening track.
layer_12_breathwork.guidance_text: Sit tall. Breathe in through the nose for four counts, feeling energy rise through the chest. Brief hold for two. Exhale through the mouth for four counts with gentle force, like stoking a flame.

layer_13_narrative.text:
24 drops of tea tree carry the dominant Terpinolene through the room brightly — the way Briceland Forest Farm intended when they cultivated XJ-13 in Briceland, Humboldt County. The lineage reads Jack Herer X G13 Haze, but what arrives in the glass is something that transcends parentage: pine needles, citrus, and bubblegum.

The second oil — Sweet Basil — adds 6 drops of brightness that matches the citrus note in the flower itself. Energized is not a command here but an invitation, the way the best strains suggest rather than insist. The body awakens into creative with the kind of molecular precision that only terpenes at 2.51% total can deliver.

In the sun-filled space, the essential oil blend has been working for thirty minutes before the flower arrives. The pre-load creates a terpene environment that the cannabis completes rather than initiates — your olfactory system already tuned to the frequency of Terpinolene, already primed for laser-focused creative energy with forest clarity. The third oil adds depth. The fourth oil rounds everything into a finish that feels inevitable rather than constructed.

This is what Briceland Forest Farm grows in Briceland, Humboldt County: not just cannabis, but context. The sativa genetics express through terpenes that speak the same chemical language as bay laurel on a Humboldt hillside. The session awakens into its final form — focused, unhurried, complete — and the 4 oils that started it are now indistinguishable from the flower that finished it.

layer_14_location.terpene_walk: Begin at Mount Tamalpais State Park, diffuse essential oil blend 30 minutes before arrival. Walk slowly, breathing deeply through the nose to layer forest phytoncides over the pre-loaded terpene profile.

wine.terpene_bridge: Gewürztraminer carries terpinolene-adjacent aromatics — lychee, rose, and allspice that mirror the fresh, herbal quality.
Riesling's terpene-rich profile carries floral and citrus compounds that harmonize with terpinolene's bright complexity.
wine.tasting_note: White peach, lime blossom, slate minerality

beer.terpene_bridge: Saaz hops carry terpinolene, and saison yeast produces spicy, herbal phenolics that amplify the terpene's complexity.
Noble hops carry terpinolene in their essential oil profile — clean, herbal, slightly floral.

layer_15_wine_beer.cannabaceae_note: Cannabis and hops (Humulus lupulus) are botanical cousins in the family Cannabaceae, sharing terpene synthesis pathways that produce identical aromatic molecules.
